contract.independentOracle: A direct Factorio physical meter independently observes the loaded fixture before accepting a result.
contract.resetModel: Reload the paired source and destination golden saves at the batch boundary; never repair a consumed fixture.

labs.title: Belts and transport lines
labs.purpose: Visualize lane-side fidelity, native stacks, corners, splitters, undergrounds, and known loss replays.

labs.title: Paired-reads census
labs.purpose: Ground the per-entity paired-read census meter: stall budget and serialize-vs-physical commensurability at the export gate.

labs.title: Engine version repin
labs.purpose: Re-certify engine-sensitive runtime shapes and behavioral rungs after a Factorio version change.

labs.title: Fluid systems
labs.purpose: Inspect fluidbox ownership, frozen reads, pumps, plasma, and exact fluid accounting.

labs.title: Validation gate drift
labs.purpose: Exercise frozen-gate timing and detect source or destination state that escapes the exact verdict.

labs.title: Hidden state semantics
labs.purpose: Probe state that is structurally present but easy for serializers or validators to miss.

labs.title: Destination hold completeness
labs.purpose: Demonstrate which entities and operations must remain frozen until transfer validation completes.

labs.title: Inserter held stacks
labs.purpose: Visualize held-item reachability, quality, filters, and busy-state round trips.

labs.title: Mid-craft machines
labs.purpose: Inspect recipe progress, ingredient embodiment, and partially completed machine state.

labs.title: No-tick synchronization
labs.purpose: Prove restoration and validation behavior while the game is paused and no normal tick advances.

labs.title: Operational tails
labs.purpose: Exercise cleanup, retry, timeout, and transaction-tail behavior around completed operations.

labs.title: Specialized inventories
labs.purpose: Map reachable inventories and fluids owned by specialized entity handlers.

labs.title: Serialized state dimensions
labs.purpose: Audit energy, temperature, burner, bonus progress, equipment, and other player-visible state dimensions.

fixtures.name: 5x5 unstacked belt loop
fixtures.purpose: Preserve 125 one-item iron-plate stacks split 67/58 across the two physical sides.
fixtures.invariant: The source loop contains exactly 125 physical one-item iron-plate stacks, 67 on side one and 58 on side two.

fixtures.name: Specialized fluid reachability
fixtures.purpose: Recertify platform-reachable specialized fluid state from prototype and live entity controls.
fixtures.note: Revision 2: the drill was recreated after an asteroid loss and all platform entities are now destructible=false (asteroid-hardening).
fixtures.invariant: Space-platform pressure/gravity are zero and the baked electric mining drill has no live recoverable fluidbox; every fixture entity is destructible=false.

fixtures.name: Adversarial nested inventory + quality
fixtures.purpose: Preserve a steel-chest holding a power-armor-mk2 whose equipment grid carries a legendary battery and uncommon shield, beside an uncommon-quality iron-gear-wheel recipe.
fixtures.invariant: The chest holds a power-armor-mk2 with a legendary battery-mk2-equipment (energy 5000000) and an uncommon energy-shield-mk2-equipment (shield 97, max 195); the assembling-machine-2 runs the iron-gear-wheel recipe at uncommon quality.

fixtures.name: Heat-pipe temperature
fixtures.purpose: Preserve a heat-pipe at a non-default temperature.
fixtures.invariant: The heat-pipe holds temperature 500.

fixtures.name: Self-feeding decider latch
fixtures.purpose: Preserve a decider-combinator latched on by its own red-wire self-feedback.
fixtures.invariant: The decider-combinator's red output network carries signal-S = 1 from its own feedback.

fixtures.name: Mid-craft assembling machine
fixtures.purpose: Preserve a deactivated assembling-machine-1 frozen partway through a craft with embodied ingredients.
fixtures.invariant: The assembling-machine-1 is inactive with crafting_progress ~0.70 and 2 iron-plate embodied in its input inventory.

fixtures.name: Burner inserter fuel state
fixtures.purpose: Preserve a deactivated burner-inserter mid-burn with a partly-consumed fuel item and fuel inventory.
fixtures.invariant: The burner-inserter is inactive, holds 10 coal, is currently burning solid-fuel, and has remaining_burning_fuel 2000000.

fixtures.name: Spidertron equipment grid
fixtures.purpose: Preserve a spidertron whose equipment grid carries a partly-charged battery.
fixtures.invariant: The spidertron's grid holds a battery-mk2-equipment charged to 50000000 of 100000000.

fixtures.name: Circuit configuration
fixtures.purpose: Preserve a constant-combinator section signal, a wired decider, and a color-using lamp.
fixtures.invariant: The constant-combinator's first section slot sets signal-A to 42 and the small-lamp uses colors.

fixtures.name: Module bonus progress
fixtures.purpose: Preserve a deactivated assembling-machine-2 with productivity modules mid bonus progress.
fixtures.invariant: The assembling-machine-2 is inactive with bonus_progress 0.5 and 2 productivity-module modules running the iron-gear-wheel recipe.

fixtures.name: Crafting-machine fluids
fixtures.purpose: Preserve a storage-tank of steam beside an inactive chemical-plant and foundry holding recipe fluids.
fixtures.invariant: The storage-tank holds 20000 steam at 500C; the chemical-plant holds 60 water and 60 petroleum-gas; the foundry holds 250 molten-iron at 1500C.

fixtures.name: Ghosts and item-request proxy
fixtures.purpose: Preserve one entity-ghost, one tile-ghost, and one item-request-proxy on a host machine.
fixtures.invariant: The platform carries exactly 1 entity-ghost (assembling-machine-2), 1 tile-ghost, and 1 item-request-proxy.

fixtures.name: Loose ground items
fixtures.purpose: Preserve loose item-entities lying on the platform floor.
fixtures.invariant: Exactly 50 iron-plate lie on the platform floor as item-entities.

fixtures.name: Platform schedule and interrupt
fixtures.purpose: Preserve the platform-level schedule records and a named interrupt.
fixtures.invariant: The platform schedule carries 2 records and 1 interrupt named 'lab-interrupt'.

fixtures.name: Accumulator drain isolation
fixtures.purpose: Preserve a charged accumulator that is the sole electric entity, documenting the drain law.
fixtures.note: Drain law: active=false does NOT stop accumulator discharge; a ~5kW deactivated machine measurably bled a deactivated accumulator, so the fixture carries no other electric entity.
fixtures.invariant: The accumulator holds energy 3000000 and is the sole electric entity on the platform; on a generation-free platform any consumer would bleed it, so it is kept alone (drain law).

fixtures.name: Over-packed belt corner
fixtures.purpose: Preserve an over-compressed turbo-belt left corner, the recovery-test replay state.
fixtures.invariant: Eight turbo-transport-belts carry 65 iron-plate total; the corner belt at (16.5,0.5) has belt_shape 'left' with 2 items over-packed on its 0.4140625-long inside lane.

fixtures.name: 1359-entity transfer workhorse
fixtures.purpose: Provide the large live-drift transfer workhorse whose structure is fixed but whose machine item counts drift by design.
fixtures.note: Structure-only fingerprint. Machines are live: measured iron-plate drifted 1688 -> 1680 over hours; only entity count is stable.
fixtures.invariant: The platform carries exactly 1359 entities; its machines are deliberately LIVE so item counts drift over time and must never be fingerprinted.

fixtures.name: Census fusion shared-plasma commensurability
fixtures.purpose: Preserve a frozen fusion reactor + generator whose parked plasma and buffer-class coolant are physically present yet visible only through buffer-aware reads - the adversarial state for paired-reads census commensurability on engine-owned fluids.
fixtures.note: Script-built by tests/lab-gallery/seed-prep-ops.lua (build_census_fusion): ignite with full 1000 coolant + 10 MW throttled load, run until parked stock >= 5, freeze. Plasma parks GENERATOR-side in the isolated steady state (reactor box empty); the live full_output state parks it reactor-side - both satisfy the law.
fixtures.invariant: Physically present fusion-plasma (engine-owned, generator nil-seg buffer) and buffer-class coolant (reactor box with segment ID whose contents read empty) are captured, counted, and restored commensurately: a transfer passes the census with zero fluid delta and the strict gate exactly; the plasma is never serialized and never restored.

testCard.law: Paired-reads census is commensurate on engine-owned fluids: transfer of a platform with live fusion plasma in shared/buffer segments yields census ok=true, zero fluid delta (fix 356c3c3; buffer-law + claim-discipline PR #120/b81a450). Teeth: a REAL omission still aborts.
testCard.build: lab-census-fusion-v1: fusion reactor (0,0) + generator (0.5,-5.5), 4 fuel cells, 990 coolant, ~5 plasma parked generator-side; all frozen + indestructible; temps removed; platform paused.
testCard.action: Production /transfer-platform to the destination instance (census in the export walk; two-phase commit). Variant B: reload pair, arm test_force_census_omission, transfer again.
testCard.expected: Census ok, fluid_delta empty; strict gate SUCCESS; coolant + fuel exact at destination (independent physical count); plasma absent at destination and validation passes; source deleted; fingerprint reproduces from the reloaded save. Variant B: abort, one attributed row, destination never contacted, source preserved.
testCard.forbidden: Phantom census abort (plasma or thruster-style delta with zero item rows); plasma in serialized totals; census green over real coolant loss; armed omission hook not aborting; leftovers the golden-pair reload does not reset.

fixtures.name: Held-item capacity on a less-researched destination
fixtures.purpose: Preserve a frozen legendary bulk-inserter whose hand is seated with 8 railgun-ammo under a baked >=11 player-force bulk-inserter capacity bonus - the adversarial state for dest-force research replication on import.
fixtures.owningRunnerWaiver: batch runner lands after this bake; until then the selection-lab drive sequence is the manual runner
fixtures.note: Script-built by tests/lab-gallery/seed-prep-ops.lua (build_inserter_held) onto the stamped inserter-held-capacity cell at (34,-128): raise the player force bulk_inserter_capacity_bonus to >=11 (raise-only, logged), create a legendary bulk-inserter at (40.5,-122.5), seat 8 railgun-ammo via plain held_stack.set_stack (no active toggle; inserter-lab B6), then freeze (active=false, destructible=false). forceBulkBonus is a placeholder floor; the live bake pins the exact baked value.
fixtures.invariant: A legendary bulk-inserter holds 8 railgun-ammo in its hand while inactive and indestructible; the player force bulk_inserter_capacity_bonus is >= 11 so the hand seats in full and survives save/load.

testCard.law: Held items seat IN FULL on a less-researched destination: export captures the source-force inserter bonuses, import replicates them raise-only, and the strict gate counts the complete state (Pitfalls #28 count-a-complete-state / #29 dest-force research).
testCard.build: lab-omnibus-state-v1 inserter-held-capacity cell (34,-128): a legendary bulk-inserter at (40.5,-122.5) holds 8 railgun-ammo via plain held_stack.set_stack (no active toggle); the player force bulk_inserter_capacity_bonus is baked >= 11; the inserter is frozen (active=false, destructible=false).
testCard.action: Production /transfer-platform of the omnibus platform to a destination whose bulk_inserter_capacity_bonus is 0 (asserted, never forced).
testCard.expected: A forceDataMismatches warning fires; the destination bonus is raised, never lowered; the hand seats 8/8 by physical count; the strict gate reports SUCCESS.
testCard.forbidden: An under-seated hand; a gate pass over a short hand; any destination bonus lowered; the baked bonus not surviving save/load.

fixtures.name: No-tick synchronous held-item pair
fixtures.purpose: Preserve a frozen mid-craft assembling-machine-1 beside an inactive empty-handed inserter - the construct-free target proving the synchronous held-item pass advances no tick.
fixtures.owningRunnerWaiver: batch runner lands after this bake; until then the selection-lab drive sequence is the manual runner
fixtures.note: Script-built by tests/lab-gallery/seed-prep-ops.lua (build_no_tick_pair) onto the stamped no-tick-sync-frozen-pair cell at (34,-114): an assembling-machine-1 at (39.5,-108.5) set to iron-gear-wheel with 4 iron-plate in crafter_input and crafting_progress write-asserted to 0.42, then frozen; a plain west-facing inserter at (42.5,-108.5), empty-handed, frozen. progress absorbs a crafting-progress ULP on save/load.
fixtures.invariant: The assembling-machine-1 is inactive with crafting_progress ~0.42, iron-gear-wheel set, and 4 iron-plate embodied; the neighboring inserter is inactive with an empty hand; every fixture entity is destructible=false.

testCard.law: The synchronous held-item pass leaves game.tick, crafting_progress, and machine inputs UNCHANGED while it seats the inserter hand, reproduced from a save-loaded world (no-tick-sync-lab).
testCard.build: lab-omnibus-state-v1 no-tick-sync-frozen-pair cell (34,-114): an inactive assembling-machine-1 at (39.5,-108.5) frozen mid-craft (iron-gear-wheel, 4 iron-plate embodied, crafting_progress 0.42 write-asserted) beside an inactive west-facing empty-handed inserter at (42.5,-108.5); both destructible=false.
testCard.action: Run the no_tick_sync_selftest remote in measure-baked-fixture (construct-free) mode against this pair under tick-pause - the strongest form.
testCard.expected: game.tick unchanged; crafting_progress exactly 0.42 after the pass; the hand seats in full.
testCard.forbidden: Any tick advance during the pass; crafting_progress drift; the selftest constructing anything on the golden world.

fixtures.name: Single-use consumable hub 1
fixtures.purpose: Provide a bare single-use platform (hub only) for the single-use batch lifecycle.
fixtures.owningRunnerWaiver: Bare single-use hub owned by the single-use batch lifecycle, not a single integration runner.
fixtures.invariant: The platform carries exactly 1 entity, the space-platform-hub, and is never repaired or reused within a batch.

fixtures.invariant: The platform carries exactly 1 entity, the space-platform-hub, and is never repaired or reused within a batch.
